blocks.body: Each, every, all are three words that mean "each" or "all", but are used differently. Each - each separately (individually), every - each without exception (as a group), all - all together (generality).

{ "title": "9. Brief synopsis", "items": [ "Each - each separately (individually). Each + singular.", "Every - everyone without exception (as a group). Every + singular.", "All - all together (generality). All + plural.", "Each/Every + singular noun & verb (is, has, does).", "All + plural noun & verb (are, have, do).", "Each of / All of + the/these/my... But NOT 'every of'!", "Each — flexible position (before, after, at the end). Every - only before a noun.", "Each - for small groups. Every - for any size." ] }
